title: A Smart Enterprise in the Port Zone
article_text: An unknown governor once owned a smart enterprise near the port zone. The elderly owner owed a large debt to a contemporary bank. He had restricted access to his solid framework because he was upset about the money. An arrival at the gate traced the problem back to a missing inch of paperwork. The equivalent of the debt was a uniform for each worker, which he had bought. He wished he had not bought the beer for the party last week. His tooth ached from the stress, but he eased the pain with a cold drink. The pitch of his voice was low when he spoke to the manager. A representation of the debt was found in a layer of old files. If he had found the trace earlier, he would have saved his enterprise from failure.

sentences.zh: 一位不知名的州长曾经在港口区域附近拥有一家聪明的企业。

image_prompts: A large port with ships and a sign saying 'Zone' in the background, an unknown governor standing near a smart enterprise building.
An elderly man sitting at a desk looking worried, with a bank note on the table.
A solid framework of a building with a sign saying 'Restricted Access', a man looking upset.
A person arriving at a gate, holding a paper that is one inch short, looking at the missing part.
A pile of uniforms on a table with a price tag showing the equivalent of a debt.
A man with a sad face holding a beer bottle, thinking about a party.
A person holding a cold drink to ease a toothache, with a pained expression.
A manager and a worker speaking, with a low pitch indicated by a sound wave.
A layer of old files on a desk, with a paper showing a representation of a debt.
A man finding a small trace on a document, looking relieved, with an enterprise building in the background.
